# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Info for Release Tracking

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  prepare_for_notification:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Store PR Title and Description for Notification Systems
        run: |
          # The PR title is single-line and straightforward.
          echo "PR_TITLE=${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" >> $GITHUB_ENV

          # The PR body can be multi-line and contain special characters.
          # Using a multi-line delimiter ensures it's stored correctly.
          echo "PR_BODY<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_ENV
        id: set_pr_info

      - name: Display PR Info for Audit Log
        run: |
          echo "PR Title: '$PR_TITLE'"
          echo "PR Description:"
          echo "$PR_BODY"

      # This is a placeholder step. Replace it with your actual notification system.
      # Example: A Slack notification using a third-party action.
      # - name: Send Build Notification (Slack Example)
      #   uses: rtCamp/action-slack-notify@v2
      #   if: success() || failure()
      #   env:
      #     SLACK_WEBHOOK: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK }} # Store your Slack webhook as a GitHub Secret
      #     SLACK_MESSAGE: |
      #       *New Pull Request:*
      #       *Title:* ${{ env.PR_TITLE }}
      #       *Description:*
      #       ${{ env.PR_BODY }}
      #       *Link:* ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
      #     SLACK_TITLE: "Feature Deployment Tracking"
      #     SLACK_COLOR: '#007ACC' # A nice blue color
      #     SLACK_USERNAME: 'GitHub Actions'
      #     SLACK_ICON_EMOJI: ':octocat:'

      # This is a placeholder for any build/test steps.
      # You would typically run your build, tests, or other checks here.
      # The PR_TITLE environment variable is available for use in these steps
      # if you need to pass it to build scripts or other tools.
      # - name: Run Build and Tests
      #   run: |
      #     echo "Building project related to feature: $PR_TITLE"
      #     # yarn install
      #     # yarn build
      #     # yarn test
      #   env:
      #     FEATURE_PR_TITLE: ${{ env.PR_TITLE }} # Example of passing to an environment variable for a script<ctrl63>
